# solution.py
"""ProjectSynergy / Team Collaboration Manager.

This module implements an in-memory, thread-safe team collaboration backend.
While the original brief framed the deliverable as a "ProjectSynergy"
management game, the concrete, testable contract is a
``TeamCollaborationManager`` that coordinates users, projects, tasks,
messaging, performance dashboards and exportable reports.

The implementation favours:

* **Correctness** - strict validation with a clear exception hierarchy.
* **Isolation** - every accessor returns defensive copies so callers can
  never mutate internal state.
* **Thread-safety** - a single reentrant lock guards every operation and is
  shared by all logical sub-stores.

Standard library only, ASCII only.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import csv
import io
import threading
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle states for a task."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class UserRole(Enum):
    """Roles a user may hold within the system."""

    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Supported export formats for reports."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """Identifiers describing the kind of report produced."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for all domain errors raised by this module."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input fails validation."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an operation."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint would be violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with the current state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain entities
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class Attachment(object):
    """A file attached to a message.

    Only metadata is retained; the raw payload is not stored beyond its size.
    """

    __slots__ = ("id", "filename", "size", "uploaded_by", "uploaded_at")

    def __init__(self, id, filename, size, uploaded_by, uploaded_at):
        self.id = id
        self.filename = filename
        self.size = size
        self.uploaded_by = uploaded_by
        self.uploaded_at = uploaded_at

    def clone(self):
        return Attachment(
            self.id,
            self.filename,
            self.size,
            self.uploaded_by,
            self.uploaded_at,
        )

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - cosmetic
        return "Attachment(id={0!r}, filename={1!r}, size={2!r})".format(
            self.id, self.filename, self.size
        )


class User(object):
    """A registered participant."""

    __slots__ = ("id", "username", "email", "role", "created_at")

    def __init__(self, id, username, email, role, created_at):
        self.id = id
        self.username = username
        self.email = email
        self.role = role
        self.created_at = created_at

    def clone(self):
        return User(self.id, self.username, self.email, self.role, self.created_at)

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - cosmetic
        return "User(id={0!r}, username={1!r}, role={2!r})".format(
            self.id, self.username, self.role
        )


class Project(object):
    """A unit of work owned by a user and containing tasks."""

    __slots__ = (
        "id",
        "name",
        "description",
        "start_date",
        "end_date",
        "owner_id",
        "created_at",
    )

    def __init__(
        self, id, name, description, start_date, end_date, owner_id, created_at
    ):
        self.id = id
        self.name = name
        self.description = description
        self.start_date = start_date
        self.end_date = end_date
        self.owner_id = owner_id
        self.created_at = created_at

    def clone(self):
        return Project(
            self.id,
            self.name,
            self.description,
            self.start_date,
            self.end_date,
            self.owner_id,
            self.created_at,
        )

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - cosmetic
        return "Project(id={0!r}, name={1!r})".format(self.id, self.name)


class Task(object):
    """A discrete piece of work belonging to a project."""

    __slots__ = (
        "id",
        "project_id",
        "title",
        "description",
        "assignee_id",
        "deadline",
        "status",
        "started_at",
        "completed_at",
        "created_at",
    )

    def __init__(
        self,
        id,
        project_id,
        title,
        description,
        assignee_id,
        deadline,
        status,
        created_at,
    ):
        self.id = id
        self.project_id = project_id
        self.title = title
        self.description = description
        self.assignee_id = assignee_id
        self.deadline = deadline
        self.status = status
        self.started_at = None
        self.completed_at = None
        self.created_at = created_at

    def clone(self):
        clone = Task(
            self.id,
            self.project_id,
            self.title,
            self.description,
            self.assignee_id,
            self.deadline,
            self.status,
            self.created_at,
        )
        clone.started_at = self.started_at
        clone.completed_at = self.completed_at
        return clone

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - cosmetic
        return "Task(id={0!r}, title={1!r}, status={2!r})".format(
            self.id, self.title, self.status
        )


class Message(object):
    """A message posted against a project or a task."""

    __slots__ = (
        "id",
        "author_id",
        "body",
        "project_id",
        "task_id",
        "parent_id",
        "attachments",
        "created_at",
        "edited_at",
    )

    def __init__(
        self,
        id,
        author_id,
        body,
        project_id,
        task_id,
        parent_id,
        created_at,
    ):
        self.id = id
        self.author_id = author_id
        self.body = body
        self.project_id = project_id
        self.task_id = task_id
        self.parent_id = parent_id
        self.attachments = []
        self.created_at = created_at
        self.edited_at = None

    def clone(self):
        clone = Message(
            self.id,
            self.author_id,
            self.body,
            self.project_id,
            self.task_id,
            self.parent_id,
            self.created_at,
        )
        clone.attachments = [att.clone() for att in self.attachments]
        clone.edited_at = self.edited_at
        return clone

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - cosmetic
        return "Message(id={0!r}, author_id={1!r})".format(self.id, self.author_id)


class Rating(object):
    """A peer rating given by one user to another."""

    __slots__ = (
        "id",
        "rater_id",
        "ratee_id",
        "score",
        "feedback",
        "task_id",
        "created_at",
    )

    def __init__(self, id, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback, task_id, created_at):
        self.id = id
        self.rater_id = rater_id
        self.ratee_id = ratee_id
        self.score = score
        self.feedback = feedback
        self.task_id = task_id
        self.created_at = created_at

    def clone(self):
        return Rating(
            self.id,
            self.rater_id,
            self.ratee_id,
            self.score,
            self.feedback,
            self.task_id,
            self.created_at,
        )

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - cosmetic
        return "Rating(id={0!r}, score={1!r})".format(self.id, self.score)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Logical sub-store
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _Component(object):
    """A lightweight namespace that shares the manager's lock.

    The manager exposes one component per logical concern (users, projects,
    tasks, messages, performance, reports).  Every component shares the *same*
    lock instance as the manager, which guarantees that operations spanning
    multiple components remain mutually exclusive.
    """

    __slots__ = ("_lock",)

    def __init__(self, lock):
        self._lock = lock


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Manager
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager(object):
    """Thread-safe, in-memory team collaboration backend.

    Parameters
    ----------
    now:
        Optional zero-argument callable returning the "current" ``datetime``.
        Injecting a deterministic clock makes time-dependent behaviour fully
        testable.  Defaults to :func:`datetime.datetime.now`.
    """

    def __init__(self, now=None):
        self._now = now if now is not None else datetime.now

        # A single reentrant lock guards everything and is shared with each
        # logical component so cross-component operations stay atomic.
        self._lock = threading.RLock()

        # Logical components (share the lock; data lives on the manager).
        self.users = _Component(self._lock)
        self.projects = _Component(self._lock)
        self.tasks = _Component(self._lock)
        self.messages = _Component(self._lock)
        self.performance = _Component(self._lock)
        self.reports = _Component(self._lock)

        # Primary stores keyed by id.
        self._users = {}
        self._projects = {}
        self._tasks = {}
        self._messages = {}
        self._ratings = []

        # Uniqueness indexes for users.
        self._username_to_id = {}
        self._email_to_id = {}

        # Globally unique, monotonically increasing id counter.
        self._id_counter = 0

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Internal helpers (assume the lock is held)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def _next_id(self):
        with self._lock:
            self._id_counter += 1
            return self._id_counter

    def _require_user(self, user_id):
        user = self._users.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user

    def _require_project(self, project_id):
        project = self._projects.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project

    def _require_task(self, task_id):
        task = self._tasks.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task

    def _require_message(self, message_id):
        message = self._messages.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message

    @staticmethod
    def _is_blank(text):
        return text is None or str(text).strip() == ""

    @staticmethod
    def _valid_email(email):
        return bool(email) and "@" in email

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # User management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def register_user(self, username, email, role=UserRole.MEMBER):
        with self._lock:
            if self._is_blank(username):
                raise ValidationError("username must not be empty")
            if not self._valid_email(email):
                raise ValidationError("email is invalid")
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
            if username in self._username_to_id:
                raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
            if email in self._email_to_id:
                raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            user = User(self._next_id(), username, email, role, self._now())
            self._users[user.id] = user
            self._username_to_id[username] = user.id
            self._email_to_id[email] = user.id
            return user.clone()

    def get_user(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            return self._require_user(user_id).clone()

    def list_users(self):
        with self._lock:
            return [self._users[uid].clone() for uid in sorted(self._users)]

    def update_user(self, user_id, username=None, email=None, role=None):
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            if username is not None:
                if self._is_blank(username):
                    raise ValidationError("username must not be empty")
                existing = self._username_to_id.get(username)
                if existing is not None and existing != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("username already in use")

            if email is not None:
                if not self._valid_email(email):
                    raise ValidationError("email is invalid")
                existing = self._email_to_id.get(email)
                if existing is not None and existing != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            if role is not None and not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")

            if username is not None and username != user.username:
                self._username_to_id.pop(user.username, None)
                self._username_to_id[username] = user_id
                user.username = username
            if email is not None and email != user.email:
                self._email_to_id.pop(user.email, None)
                self._email_to_id[email] = user_id
                user.email = email
            if role is not None:
                user.role = role

            return user.clone()

    def delete_user(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)

            for project in self._projects.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:
                    raise ConflictError("user owns a project")

            for task in self._tasks.values():
                if (
                    task.assignee_id == user_id
                    and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                ):
                    raise ConflictError("user has open assigned tasks")

            # Detach the user from any completed tasks they were assigned.
            for task in self._tasks.values():
                if task.assignee_id == user_id:
                    task.assignee_id = None

            user = self._users.pop(user_id)
            self._username_to_id.pop(user.username, None)
            self._email_to_id.pop(user.email, None)
            return None

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Project management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def create_project(self, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description=""):
        with self._lock:
            if self._is_blank(name):
                raise ValidationError("project name must not be empty")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must not precede start_date")
            self._require_user(owner_id)

            project = Project(
                self._next_id(),
                name,
                description or "",
                start_date,
                end_date,
                owner_id,
                self._now(),
            )
            self._projects[project.id] = project
            return project.clone()

    def get_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            return self._require_project(project_id).clone()

    def list_projects(self):
        with self._lock:
            return [self._projects[pid].clone() for pid in sorted(self._projects)]

    def update_project(
        self, project_id, name=None, start_date=None, end_date=None, description=None
    ):
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)

            if name is not None and self._is_blank(name):
                raise ValidationError("project name must not be empty")

            effective_start = start_date if start_date is not None else project.start_date
            effective_end = end_date if end_date is not None else project.end_date
            if effective_end < effective_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must not precede start_date")

            if name is not None:
                project.name = name
            if start_date is not None:
                project.start_date = start_date
            if end_date is not None:
                project.end_date = end_date
            if description is not None:
                project.description = description

            return project.clone()

    def delete_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)

            task_ids = [
                tid
                for tid, task in self._tasks.items()
                if task.project_id == project_id
            ]
            for tid in task_ids:
                del self._tasks[tid]

            removed_tasks = set(task_ids)
            message_ids = [
                mid
                for mid, msg in self._messages.items()
                if msg.project_id == project_id or msg.task_id in removed_tasks
            ]
            for mid in message_ids:
                del self._messages[mid]

            del self._projects[project_id]
            return None

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Task management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def create_task(
        self, project_id, title, assignee_id=None, deadline=None, description=""
    ):
        with self._lock:
            if self._is_blank(title):
                raise ValidationError("task title must not be empty")
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._require_user(assignee_id)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline must not precede project start")

            task = Task(
                self._next_id(),
                project_id,
                title,
                description or "",
                assignee_id,
                deadline,
                TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                self._now(),
            )
            self._tasks[task.id] = task
            return task.clone()

    def get_task(self, task_id):
        with self._lock:
            return self._require_task(task_id).clone()

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            tasks = [t for t in self._tasks.values() if t.project_id == project_id]
            tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t.id)
            return [t.clone() for t in tasks]

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, assignee_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            tasks = [t for t in self._tasks.values() if t.assignee_id == assignee_id]
            tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t.id)
            return [t.clone() for t in tasks]

    def assign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            task.assignee_id = assignee_id
            return task.clone()

    def reassign_task(self, task_id, new_assignee_id):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(new_assignee_id)
            task.assignee_id = new_assignee_id
            return task.clone()

    def unassign_task(self, task_id):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            task.assignee_id = None
            return task.clone()

    def set_deadline(self, task_id, deadline):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._projects.get(task.project_id)
                if project is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline must not precede project start")
            task.deadline = deadline
            return task.clone()

    def update_status(self, task_id, status):
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            now = self._now()

            if status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now

            task.status = status
            return task.clone()

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Messaging
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id,
        body,
        project_id=None,
        task_id=None,
        parent_id=None,
        attachments=None,
    ):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(author_id)

            if (project_id is None) == (task_id is None):
                raise ValidationError(
                    "exactly one of project_id or task_id is required"
                )

            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)
            if parent_id is not None:
                self._require_message(parent_id)

            attachment_specs = list(attachments) if attachments else []
            if self._is_blank(body) and not attachment_specs:
                raise ValidationError("message body or attachment required")

            message = Message(
                self._next_id(),
                author_id,
                body,
                project_id,
                task_id,
                parent_id,
                self._now(),
            )
            for filename, data in attachment_specs:
                message.attachments.append(
                    self._make_attachment(author_id, filename, data)
                )
            self._messages[message.id] = message
            return message.clone()

    def _make_attachment(self, uploaded_by, filename, data):
        if self._is_blank(filename):
            raise ValidationError("attachment filename must not be empty")
        size = len(data) if data is not None else 0
        return Attachment(
            self._next_id(), filename, size, uploaded_by, self._now()
        )

    def edit_message(self, message_id, editor_id, new_body):
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may edit this message")
            if self._is_blank(new_body):
                raise ValidationError("message body must not be empty")
            message.body = new_body
            message.edited_at = self._now()
            return message.clone()

    def add_attachment(self, message_id, uploader_id, filename, data):
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != uploader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "only the author may attach to this message"
                )
            attachment = self._make_attachment(uploader_id, filename, data)
            message.attachments.append(attachment)
            return message.clone()

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            msgs = [m for m in self._messages.values() if m.project_id == project_id]
            msgs.sort(key=lambda m: m.id)
            return [m.clone() for m in msgs]

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_task(task_id)
            msgs = [m for m in self._messages.values() if m.task_id == task_id]
            msgs.sort(key=lambda m: m.id)
            return [m.clone() for m in msgs]

    def delete_message(self, message_id, requester_id):
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != requester_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may delete this message")
            del self._messages[message_id]
            return None

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Performance dashboard
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def _tasks_for_assignee(self, user_id):
        return [t for t in self._tasks.values() if t.assignee_id == user_id]

    @staticmethod
    def _completion_seconds(task):
        if task.started_at is not None and task.completed_at is not None:
            return (task.completed_at - task.started_at).total_seconds()
        return None

    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            return completed / len(tasks)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            durations = []
            for task in self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id):
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    seconds = self._completion_seconds(task)
                    if seconds is not None:
                        durations.append(seconds)
            if not durations:
                return None
            return sum(durations) / len(durations)

    def submit_rating(self, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback="", task_id=None):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(rater_id)
            self._require_user(ratee_id)
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("users cannot rate themselves")
            if isinstance(score, bool) or not isinstance(score, int):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer between 1 and 5")
            if score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be between 1 and 5")
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)

            rating = Rating(
                self._next_id(),
                rater_id,
                ratee_id,
                score,
                feedback or "",
                task_id,
                self._now(),
            )
            self._ratings.append(rating)
            return rating.clone()

    def _ratings_for(self, user_id):
        return [r for r in self._ratings if r.ratee_id == user_id]

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": [r.clone() for r in ratings],
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            completion_rate = completed / total if total else 0.0

            durations = [
                self._completion_seconds(t)
                for t in tasks
                if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                and self._completion_seconds(t) is not None
            ]
            average_completion_time = (
                sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
            )

            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count
                else 0.0
            )

            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": completion_rate,
                "average_completion_time": average_completion_time,
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Reports
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def _user_metrics(self, user_id, tasks):
        total = len(tasks)
        completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
        completion_rate = completed / total if total else 0.0
        durations = [
            self._completion_seconds(t)
            for t in tasks
            if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            and self._completion_seconds(t) is not None
        ]
        average_completion_time = (
            sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
        )
        ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
        rating_count = len(ratings)
        rating_average = (
            sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count if rating_count else 0.0
        )
        user = self._users.get(user_id)
        return {
            "user_id": user_id,
            "username": user.username if user is not None else None,
            "total_tasks": total,
            "completed_tasks": completed,
            "completion_rate": completion_rate,
            "average_completion_time": average_completion_time,
            "rating_count": rating_count,
            "rating_average": rating_average,
        }

    def project_progress_report(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            tasks = [t for t in self._tasks.values() if t.project_id == project_id]
            total = len(tasks)

            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
            completed = status_counts[TaskStatus.COMPLETED.value]
            completion_rate = completed / total if total else 0.0

            now = self._now()
            overdue = sum(
                1
                for t in tasks
                if t.deadline is not None
                and t.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                and now > t.deadline
            )

            per_assignee = {}
            for task in tasks:
                if task.assignee_id is None:
                    continue
                bucket = per_assignee.setdefault(
                    task.assignee_id,
                    {
                        "total": 0,
                        "completed": 0,
                        "in_progress": 0,
                        "not_started": 0,
                    },
                )
                bucket["total"] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    bucket["completed"] += 1
                elif task.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                    bucket["in_progress"] += 1
                else:
                    bucket["not_started"] += 1
            for bucket in per_assignee.values():
                bucket["completion_rate"] = (
                    bucket["completed"] / bucket["total"]
                    if bucket["total"]
                    else 0.0
                )

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": completion_rate,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id=None):
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
                scoped = [
                    t for t in self._tasks.values() if t.project_id == project_id
                ]
            else:
                scoped = list(self._tasks.values())

            user_ids = sorted(
                {t.assignee_id for t in scoped if t.assignee_id is not None}
            )
            rows = []
            for uid in user_ids:
                user_tasks = [t for t in scoped if t.assignee_id == uid]
                rows.append(self._user_metrics(uid, user_tasks))

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t.id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            completion_rate = completed / total if total else 0.0

            durations = [
                self._completion_seconds(t)
                for t in tasks
                if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                and self._completion_seconds(t) is not None
            ]
            average_completion_time = (
                sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
            )

            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count
                else 0.0
            )

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "username": user.username,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": completion_rate,
                "average_completion_time": average_completion_time,
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
                "tasks": [t.clone() for t in tasks],
            }

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Report export
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def export_report(self, report, report_format):
        if not isinstance(report, dict):
            raise ValidationError("report must be a mapping")
        if not isinstance(report_format, ReportFormat):
            raise ValidationError("report_format must be a ReportFormat")

        if report_format == ReportFormat.CSV:
            return self._report_to_csv(report)
        return self._report_to_pdf(report)

    @staticmethod
    def _csv_cell(value):
        if value is None:
            return ""
        return str(value)

    def _report_to_csv(self, report):
        out = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(out)
        report_type = report.get("report_type")

        if report_type == ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value:
            columns = [
                "user_id",
                "username",
                "total_tasks",
                "completed_tasks",
                "completion_rate",
                "average_completion_time",
                "rating_count",
                "rating_average",
            ]
            writer.writerow(columns)
            for row in report.get("rows", []):
                writer.writerow([self._csv_cell(row.get(c)) for c in columns])

        elif report_type == ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value:
            columns = [
                "user_id",
                "username",
                "total_tasks",
                "completed_tasks",
                "in_progress_tasks",
                "not_started_tasks",
                "completion_rate",
                "average_completion_time",
                "rating_count",
                "rating_average",
            ]
            writer.writerow(columns)
            writer.writerow([self._csv_cell(report.get(c)) for c in columns])

        elif report_type == ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value:
            columns = [
                "project_id",
                "total_tasks",
                "completed_tasks",
                "completion_rate",
                "overdue_tasks",
            ]
            writer.writerow(columns)
            writer.writerow([self._csv_cell(report.get(c)) for c in columns])

        else:
            scalars = [
                (key, value)
                for key, value in report.items()
                if not isinstance(value, (list, dict, tuple))
            ]
            writer.writerow([key for key, _ in scalars])
            writer.writerow([self._csv_cell(value) for _, value in scalars])

        return out.getvalue()

    def _report_to_pdf(self, report):
        lines = ["Report: {0}".format(report.get("report_type", "report"))]
        for key, value in report.items():
            if isinstance(value, (list, dict, tuple)):
                lines.append("{0}: {1} item(s)".format(key, len(value)))
            else:
                lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, value))
        return self._build_pdf(lines)

    @staticmethod
    def _build_pdf(text_lines):
        """Assemble a minimal, structurally valid single-page PDF."""

        operations = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "72 720 Td", "14 TL"]
        for index, line in enumerate(text_lines):
            escaped = (
                line.replace("\\", "\\\\")
                .replace("(", "\\(")
                .replace(")", "\\)")
            )
            if index == 0:
                operations.append("({0}) Tj".format(escaped))
            else:
                operations.append("T* ({0}) Tj".format(escaped))
        operations.append("ET")
        stream = "\n".join(operations).encode("latin-1", "replace")

        objects = [
            b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
            b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
            b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
            b"/Contents 4 0 R /Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> >>",
            b"<< /Length "
            + str(len(stream)).encode("ascii")
            + b" >>\nstream\n"
            + stream
            + b"\nendstream",
            b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
        ]

        out = bytearray()
        out += b"%PDF-1.4\n"
        offsets = []
        for index, body in enumerate(objects, start=1):
            offsets.append(len(out))
            out += str(index).encode("ascii") + b" 0 obj\n" + body + b"\nendobj\n"

        xref_position = len(out)
        size = len(objects) + 1
        out += b"xref\n"
        out += b"0 " + str(size).encode("ascii") + b"\n"
        out += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
        for offset in offsets:
            out += ("%010d 00000 n \n" % offset).encode("ascii")
        out += (
            b"trailer\n<< /Size "
            + str(size).encode("ascii")
            + b" /Root 1 0 R >>\n"
        )
        out += b"startxref\n" + str(xref_position).encode("ascii") + b"\n%%EOF"
        return bytes(out)
